description: Animation Quality and Play Rules - real two-frame art for every pose instead of one drawing being nudged about, a battle projectile that lands and vanishes with the flinch tied to impact, a livelier watch-face complication, battles gated by energy rather than a daily counter, a sleeping Digimon that actually wakes when prodded, per-branch evolution hints in the Dex, and a room light that dims the room. Source PRDs: tasks/prd-live-animation-and-battle-impact.md (US-102 to US-107) and tasks/prd-battle-cost-sleep-and-screen-polish.md (US-108 to US-113). Story ids continue the project's running sequence rather than restarting at US-001, so progress.txt and the source PRDs cross-reference correctly.

userStories.title: Two-frame loops for the poses that were held still
userStories.description: As a developer, I need SpriteAnimation to express 'this pose is a loop of the pose frame and walk1', so every screen gets moving art from the same mechanism the eat and sleep loops already use. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-live-animation-and-battle-impact.md, US-102.
acceptanceCriteria: Add ONE case to SpriteAnimation in Sources/DigimonSpriteView.swift: `case pose(SpriteFrame)`. Do NOT add four per-pose cases
`pose(frame).stageFrames` returns `[frame, .walk1]` - pose frame FIRST, walk1 second
`pose(_).frameDuration` is SpriteAnimation.frameDuration (0.5s), the shared V-Pet beat
`pose(_).eggFrames` is `[]`, so a Digitama falls through to the placeholder exactly as .still already does
`pose(.walk1)` yields `[.walk1, .walk1]` and needs no special-case guard
`.still(_)` is UNCHANGED and still returns a one-element stageFrames - it is still used for the dead Digimon and the battle result screen
SpriteAnimation remains Hashable and still usable in MainScreenModel.restingPoses
Test: for each of refuse, happy, angry, attack, `pose(frame).frames(from:)` on a synthetic 48x64 stage sheet returns 2 distinct CGImages and the first is the pose frame
Test: SpriteAnimation.frameIndex over a 2-frame pose loop alternates 0,1,0,1 across four consecutive 0.5s samples
Test: `pose(_).frames(from:)` on a 48x16 egg sheet returns an empty array
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added `case pose(SpriteFrame)` to SpriteAnimation: stageFrames [frame, .walk1], shared 0.5s beat, empty eggFrames; .still unchanged. 8 new SpriteAnimationTests (synthetic 48x64 sheet, 0/1/0/1 cadence, Hashable). Build green, 1264 tests pass.

userStories.title: The Digimon actually moves when it refuses, trains and celebrates
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Digimon's drawing to change while it reacts, so a refusal or a celebration reads as the creature moving rather than as one picture being shoved about the screen. Depends on US-102. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-live-animation-and-battle-impact.md, US-103.
acceptanceCriteria: MainScreenModel food refusal (~line 972) shows `.pose(.refuse)`, keeping `motion: .shake` and the 'Not hungry.' caption
MainScreenModel training outcome (~line 1066) shows `.pose(.attack)` on a gain and `.pose(.angry)` on none, keeping `motion: .lunge` / `.recoil`
MainScreenModel cleaning (~line 1117) shows `.pose(.happy)`, keeping `motion: .hop` and the 'All clean!' caption
The dead Digimon in restingPoses (~line 916) is STILL `.still(.hurt2)` and does NOT animate
restingPoses still contains every pose restingAnimation can return, so a Digimon leaving an action pose settles back correctly
ActionMotion is NOT changed - frame swapping is added alongside the displacement, not instead of it

Correct the comments that now contradict the code, do not leave them: the 'Both poses are single held frames' comment above the training show() call, and the refusal and cleaning comments that call these still poses
Test: after feed() on a full Digimon the model's animation is `.pose(.refuse)` and yields 2 frames
Test: after a training round the model's animation is `.pose(.attack)` on a gain and `.pose(.angry)` on no gain
Test: after clean() the model's animation is `.pose(.happy)`
Existing FeedMotionTests and CleanTrainMotionTests pass, updated where they assert `.still(_)`
Tests pass
Verify on the watchOS Simulator: screenshot the celebration pose at two instants 0.5s apart and confirm the sprite tile pixels DIFFER
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The three action call sites now show .pose(.refuse) / .pose(.attack) / .pose(.angry) / .pose(.happy) with their motions and captions unchanged; the dead pose is still .still(.hurt2) and ActionMotion is untouched. Contradicting comments rewritten. Build green, 1267 tests / 0 failures. Screenshot burst on the 42mm sim (-poopCleanDemo, 16 shots at 0.13s) shows the sprite tile alternating between two lit-pixel counts (3518 / 3734) in ~0.5s runs — two shots 0.575s apart differ.

userStories.title: The battle projectile vanishes on impact and never flies backward
userStories.description: As a user, I want a shot to hit the other Digimon and be gone, so the exchange reads as a blow landing instead of a glyph bouncing back to where it came from. THE BUG: in BattleView.run(), `projectileProgress = 0` sits on the line immediately before `withAnimation(...) { beat = .turn(index) }`, so SwiftUI sweeps the reset into the animated transaction and the glyph slides BACKWARD from the defender to the attacker over 0.15s. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-live-animation-and-battle-impact.md, US-104.
acceptanceCriteria: A shot is NOT drawn at all once its flight has completed - no parked glyph on the defender, no reverse travel, not for a single frame
projectileProgress is reset with animations explicitly disabled (withTransaction / disablesAnimations), so a future reordering of run() cannot resurrect the reverse slide
Impact is captured as view state (e.g. `@State private var hasLanded: Bool`) set after flightDuration elapses within the turn - US-105 hangs the hurt loop on it
hasLanded resets to false at the start of every turn
Consecutive turns by the SAME attacker each start their shot at the attacker - the case where the backward slide was most visible
Turns by ALTERNATING attackers still fly in the correct direction per BattleView.faces(_:)
flightDuration < turnDuration is still asserted; update the comment on defaultFlightDuration so it describes the tail as the beat where the defender flinches alone
Add a pure view-free predicate answering 'is the projectile visible at elapsed t of this turn', following the existing isKnockoutTurn / projectileOffset pattern
Test: the predicate is true for 0 < t < flightDuration and false for t >= flightDuration and for t <= 0
Test: driving run() at a fast injected pacing through a multi-turn report, the projectile is never visible at a progress value that DECREASED between two samples
Existing BattleTests and BattleArenaTests pass
Tests pass
Verify on the watchOS Simulator: screenshots at ~0.5x and ~1.2x flightDuration into a turn - the first shows the glyph in the gap, the second shows NO glyph anywhere in the arena
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The reset is now a withTransaction(disablesAnimations) snap of projectileProgress AND a new `hasLanded` @State, the projectile is drawn only while `!hasLanded`, and run() sleeps `flight` (flightDuration clamped to the turn) before setting it. Pure BattleView.isProjectileVisible(atElapsed:flightDuration:) is the drawing rule as arithmetic. TWO further fixes the Simulator forced: run() is now @MainActor (its three writes only coalesce into one SwiftUI update on the main thread) and the projectile carries .id(index) — without them the animated `beat` change interpolated ONE reused glyph from the old turn's tint and offset to the new one's, photographed mid-gap at the turn boundary. 7 new BattleProjectileTests; build green, 1274 tests / 0 failures. On the 42mm sim (-battleTurnDemo, flight 12s, turn 60s): glyph in the gap at 0.52x flight, nothing anywhere at 1.22x; 30 in-flight frames advance monotonically and never come back; two turn boundaries burst at 0.17s (136 frames) show an empty gap through the crossfade.

userStories.title: The defender flinches on impact, and the attacker's swing animates
userStories.description: As a user, I want the hit to visibly cause the flinch, and the attacker's swing to be a moving drawing rather than one frame held for the whole exchange. Today the defender starts flinching 1.1s before anything reaches it. Depends on US-102 and US-104. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-live-animation-and-battle-impact.md, US-105.
acceptanceCriteria: BattleView.animation(for:during:) gains a landed parameter and stays STATIC and PURE, so the whole mapping is assertable without building a view
Attacker plays `.pose(.attack)` for the whole turn, both before and after impact
Defender plays `.idle` BEFORE impact and `.hurt` (hurt1 <-> hurt2) AFTER impact
The defender's hurt loop is visibly two alternating frames during the post-impact tail, not one frame held
Turn 2's defender does not begin already flinching - it reads the reset hasLanded from US-104
The result screen is UNCHANGED: `.still(.happy)` on a win and `.still(.hurt1)` on a loss stay HELD, because the fight is over
Rewrite the BattleView doc comment that says 'the ATTACKER holds the attack frame (11)' to describe the loop instead
Test: animation(for: .player, during: turnWherePlayerAttacks, landed: false) is `.pose(.attack)`, and the same with landed: true is also `.pose(.attack)`
Test: the defender's animation is `.idle` at landed: false and `.hurt` at landed: true, for BOTH BattleSide values
Test: across a real multi-turn BattleReport, every turn's attacker/defender assignment matches turn.attacker
Tests pass
Verify on the watchOS Simulator: screenshots of the defender at two instants ~0.5s apart AFTER impact show differing sprite pixels
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: animation(for:during:landed:) is pure and static: attacker .pose(.attack) throughout, defender .idle before impact and .hurt after, keyed off US-104's hasLanded. Result screen still .still(.happy)/.still(.hurt1). 7 new tests in Tests/BattleFlinchTests.swift; build green, 1281 tests 0 failures. Simulator 42mm, -battleTurnDemo: the defender tile alternates two drawings every ~0.52s after impact (1764 pixels differ, lit count constant), and the idle loop's 2592-lit frame never appears after impact.

userStories.title: Delete the US-048 measurement spike
userStories.description: As a user, I do not want a debug measurement widget offering itself on my watch face; as a developer, I do not want dead instrument code a future iteration might mistake for a feature. The file's own header says to delete it once US-049 landed. US-049 landed. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-live-animation-and-battle-impact.md, US-106.
acceptanceCriteria: Sources/Complication/RefreshGranularitySpike.swift is DELETED

The `#if DEBUG RefreshGranularitySpikeWidget()` entry is removed from DigiVPetComplicationBundle in Sources/Complication/DigiVPetComplication.swift, leaving DigiVPetComplication() as the only widget in the bundle
docs/widget-refresh-granularity.md is KEPT - it is the spike's actual deliverable - with a line added recording that the instrument was removed on this date and that the AOD floor is still unmeasured
`grep -rn 'RefreshGranularitySpike\|SpikeEntry\|SpikeBand' Sources Tests` returns NOTHING
Re-run `xcodegen generate` after deleting the source file
A DEBUG build succeeds - the spike was DEBUG-only, so a release build passing proves nothing
ComplicationTests still pass
Tests pass
Verify on the watchOS Simulator: the widget picker shows one Digimon complication and no 'SPIKE refresh' entry
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Deleted Sources/Complication/RefreshGranularitySpike.swift and its #if DEBUG bundle entry; grep for RefreshGranularitySpike/SpikeEntry/SpikeBand over Sources and Tests returns nothing. docs/widget-refresh-granularity.md kept, its 'deliberately still here' paragraph replaced by a removal record dated 2026-07-21 stating the AOD floor is still unmeasured. xcodegen re-run; DEBUG build green; 1281 tests 0 failures incl. ComplicationTests 27/27. Picker verified on the 42mm sim via chronod's descriptor cache: the uninstall purged BOTH DigiVPetComplication and DigiVPetRefreshSpike, and the reinstall Found exactly one descriptor - kind DigiVPetComplication, displayName Digimon - with no spike descriptor anywhere after it.

userStories.title: One-second complication cadence
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Digimon on my watch face to move at a lively pace rather than shuffling one step every five seconds. READ docs/widget-refresh-granularity.md BEFORE STARTING - it is the measurement this changes. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-live-animation-and-battle-impact.md, US-107.
acceptanceCriteria: ComplicationTimeline.frameInterval is 1 second and motionEntryCount is 300, PRESERVING the existing five-minute horizon

Do NOT keep 60 entries at 1s: that is a one-minute horizon, 1440 reloads a day, budget exhausted, and WidgetKit then freezes on the last entry - a STILL sprite, worse than the 5s walk being replaced. US-048 established a whole batch is ONE budget charge however many entries it holds, so spacing is free and horizon is not
reloadDate(for:from:) still lands one frameInterval past the last entry
Rewrite the cadence doc comment on ComplicationTimeline - it currently argues FOR 5s over 1s and would directly contradict the code
Held poses (sleeping, sick, dead, messy) still emit EXACTLY ONE entry and the heldRefreshInterval path is untouched
Test: entries(for:from:) on an animating snapshot returns 300 entries spaced exactly 1s apart, alternating step parity 0,1,0,1
Test: entries(for:from:) on each held pose returns exactly 1 entry
Test: reloadDate is 300s after start for an animating pose and heldRefreshInterval after start for a held one

Observe on the Simulator that the batch is accepted VERBATIM - chronod logs entry count 300 with the authored date range, the same reading US-048 took. If it is coalesced or truncated, RECORD the observed count and FALL BACK to 2s / 150 entries rather than forcing it, and say so in notes
docs/widget-refresh-granularity.md gains a short section recording what shipped, the entry count actually accepted, and that AOD remains unmeasured
Tests pass
Verify on the watchOS Simulator via -complicationDemo: two screenshots one second apart show DIFFERENT sprite frames
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: A battle costs energy, and the daily cap is gone
userStories.description: As a user, I want to battle as often as my Digimon has the energy for, so the limit is something I can act on by moving rather than a counter I wait out. Today a battle costs NOTHING and BattleLimits.perDay = 5 is the only brake. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-battle-cost-sleep-and-screen-polish.md, US-108.
acceptanceCriteria: BattleLimits is DELETED, along with GameState.battlesRemaining(now:calendar:), MainScreenModel.battlesRemainingToday and MainScreenModel.battleLimitReason
GameState.battlesFought(now:calendar:) is KEPT - ConditionEvaluator reads it for .day-window battle conditions and those must still work
A battle costs 5 points from Strength or Stamina, whichever the Digimon holds MORE of, ties to Strength - identical to TrainAction.energyCostPerTraining and TrainAction.payableWith
The 'charge the richest payable energy' rule has EXACTLY ONE implementation, called by both training and battling; grep finds no second copy
The cost is spent from stageEnergy ONLY and never credited back - a battle dismissed halfway has still been paid for, exactly as TrainAction.begin documents
lifetimeEnergy is untouched by a battle
A battle with insufficient energy is blocked with a message naming the remedy, in the register of TrainAction's 'Not enough Strength or Stamina. Move to earn more.'
Guard order in MainScreenModel.battle() is dead -> asleep -> funds -> matchmaking
A blocked battle opens NO minigame and spends nothing
recordBattle stays untouched: LOSING STILL COSTS NOTHING - no care mistake, no health change
The -battleLimitDemo launch argument is removed or replaced with one that EMPTIES Strength and Stamina, so the blocked state stays screenshottable

Replace BattleLimits' comment with one explaining the new brake: energy comes from real HealthKit steps and exercise minutes, so 20 battles costs 100 points of real walking where 20 taps used to cost nothing. The anti-farming protection changes form, it does not disappear
Test: SIX consecutive battles in one local day all succeed given enough energy - the case the old cap forbade
Test: a battle with 4 points in both payable energies is blocked and spends nothing
Test: 7 Strength / 6 Stamina charges Strength; 6 Strength / 7 Stamina charges Stamina; equal amounts charges Strength
Test: stageEnergy drops by exactly 5 and lifetimeEnergy is unchanged
BattleLimitTests is deleted or rewritten against the energy rule - NOT left asserting a cap that no longer exists
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: The Battle button says what it is waiting for
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Battle button to tell me why I cannot battle, so 'no battles left today' is not replaced by a button that is simply dead. Depends on US-108. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-battle-cost-sleep-and-screen-polish.md, US-109.
acceptanceCriteria: ActionControls.battlesLeft is gone; the view takes whatever US-108's rule needs (e.g. whether the Digimon can afford a battle)
Battle is disabled when the Digimon cannot afford the cost and enabled otherwise
The disabled caption names ENERGY, not a daily allowance
NO caption is shown when a battle IS affordable - a permanent cost label on one of five buttons is noise on a 41mm screen
The orange 'run out' tint applies to the unaffordable state, reusing the existing visual language rather than inventing a new one
The Train button's affordance is unchanged, and the two buttons now read consistently since they gate on the same energy
Test: isBattleDisabled is true with 4 points in both payable energies and false with 5
Test: the caption is absent when affordable and present when not
ActionControlsTests updated, not deleted
Tests pass
Verify on the watchOS Simulator: screenshots of the action row affordable and unaffordable on 41mm, with all five buttons still fitting the row
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Feeding, training or battling wakes a sleeping Digimon
userStories.description: As a user, I want prodding my sleeping Digimon to actually wake it and do the thing, so the care mistake I am charged is for a disturbance that really happened. Today MainScreenModel calls noteWakingEarly() and then BLOCKS the action - the user pays and gets nothing. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-battle-cost-sleep-and-screen-polish.md, US-110.
acceptanceCriteria: Feeding, training and battling a Digimon inside its sleep window all PROCEED - the meal is eaten, the training round opens, the battle round opens

The model wakes the Digimon FIRST (records the mistake, sets the awake marker) and THEN calls the pure action with isAsleep: false. Keep FeedAction.feed and TrainAction.begin pure and unchanged; update their doc comments to say who calls them and when, so their sleep guards do not read as dead code

Each of the three records the disturbance via the EXISTING recordWakingEarly(now:calendar:): stageSleepDisturbances increments EVERY time, careMistakeCount at most ONCE per local day. Neither cap nor counter changes in this story

GameState gains a PERSISTED awake marker (e.g. `awakeUntil: Date?`) set to now + wakeGracePeriod when a sleeping Digimon is woken. It must survive a relaunch, so it is saved state and not a @Published field on the model
wakeGracePeriod is a named constant of 5 minutes with a comment saying it is a chosen balance number
While the marker is live, isAsleep is false: the Digimon shows the walk loop, wanders (isWandering), and further actions run without a second wake
When the marker expires AND the sleep window still holds, the Digimon returns to the sleep loop with no user action

CRITICAL: updateSleepState() runs on EVERY refresh() and re-derives isAsleep from the inferred window. It MUST consult the awake marker, or every foreground silently undoes the wake. This is the most likely way this story ships broken
The marker is cleared or ignored once the sleep window has passed, so it cannot leak into the next day
A DEAD Digimon still cannot be fed, trained or battled - the death guard sits before the wake
A SICK Digimon's existing blocks are unchanged
Correct the doc comments that claim these actions are blocked while asleep, including noteWakingEarly's own comment which currently describes matching a BLOCK
Tests use the INJECTED clock and wait for NO real time
Test: feeding at 02:00 inside the window feeds, charges one mistake, and increments disturbances
Test: feed then train then battle in one night gives 3 disturbances and EXACTLY 1 care mistake
Test: disturbances on two consecutive nights give 2 care mistakes
Test: isAsleep is false at wake + 4m59s and true again at wake + 5m01s, given the window still holds
Test: a Digimon woken at 06:58 with a window ending 07:00 is simply awake at 07:01
Test: reloading the model mid-grace from a fresh store read still reports the Digimon awake
Test: three disturbed nights reach Sickness.careMistakesUntilSick and the Digimon falls ill

SleepStateTests, SleepQueryTests and CareMistakeTests updated where they assert the old blocked behaviour. Expect other fixtures spanning a sleep window to need a seed put right - comment each one with the reason, as the US-098 light fixtures were
Tests pass
Verify on the watchOS Simulator: screenshot a Digimon inside its sleep window before and after a Feed tap - the second shows the walk loop, not the sleep loop
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Tapping an evolution candidate shows that branch's conditions
userStories.description: As a user, I want to tap a '?' in the Dex and be told what THAT one needs, so the hint list answers the question I asked rather than the union of every question. This deliberately reverses the flattening decision documented in DexView - the flat list stays the DEFAULT, and the per-branch view is something the player now asks for by tapping. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-battle-cost-sleep-and-screen-polish.md, US-111.
acceptanceCriteria: DexCandidateCell is tappable for discovered AND undiscovered ('?') candidates alike - the undiscovered case is the whole point
Tapping a candidate selects it and the 'It wants' list shows ONLY that candidate's conditions, in authored order
The section heading changes while a branch is selected, so the list is not silently narrower than it looks (e.g. 'It wants' -> 'To become this')
Tapping the selected candidate again DESELECTS it and the list returns to the flat all-branches view
Tapping a different candidate moves the selection directly, with no intermediate deselect

The selected cell is visibly marked, and that marking does NOT reuse the green earned outline or the discovered/undiscovered brightness - both channels already carry meanings, and a third meaning on either would make an earned-but-unselected cell indistinguishable from a selected one
Selecting reveals NOTHING about an undiscovered candidate's name or sprite - it stays a '?' while selected
Selecting a candidate whose edge is unconditional shows a plain line saying it needs nothing, not an empty section
Selection resets when the sheet is dismissed and reopened
Deduplication across branches applies only to the flat list; a selected branch shows its own conditions untouched
ConditionReveal and ConditionHintRow are UNCHANGED - this story changes which conditions are listed, never how one is worded or how warm it reads
Rewrite the DexView comment arguing for the flat list so it explains why flat is the DEFAULT, rather than leaving it contradicting the code. Do not delete the reasoning
Test: with two branches sharing one criterion, the flat list shows it ONCE and each selected branch shows its own
Test: selecting, re-selecting and cross-selecting produce the expected condition lists, as a pure function of (selection, candidates) with no view
Test: a selected undiscovered candidate's displayName appears NOWHERE in the rendered text
Tests pass
Verify on the watchOS Simulator: screenshots on 41mm of the flat list, a selected branch, and the same branch deselected
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: The light dims the Digimon's room, not the whole screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want turning the light down to darken where the Digimon is, so the buttons and the Dex stay readable at night. NOTE: the light button is ALREADY top-leading in the sprite slot - nothing moves. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-battle-cost-sleep-and-screen-polish.md, US-112.
acceptanceCriteria: The scrim in LightLayer is confined to the sprite slot's resolved rect - the spriteSlot Anchor is already passed in and already used to place the button
The action button row, the energy bars, the name/message line and the poop pile are at FULL brightness in semi and off
The Dex toolbar button is at full brightness in every light state; remove the `.opacity(1 - model.lightState.dimOpacity)` line and its comment from ContentView
The light button is still drawn ABOVE the scrim and is still legible and tappable at off - the ordering LightLayer's doc comment calls 'the feature'
The light button has NOT moved: still top-leading of the sprite slot at LightButtonLayout.inset
The scrim still allowsHitTesting(false), so Feed / Train / Clean / Battle / the bell stay tappable through it
LightState.dimOpacity values are UNCHANGED (on 0, semi 0.5, off as authored) - this story changes the scrim's EXTENT, not its darkness
The dimDuration fade still applies and the scrim is still faded rather than removed, so it can animate
Before the first layout pass (spriteSlot == nil) the scrim draws NOTHING rather than covering the screen - a nil anchor must not fall back to full-screen
The battle, training, ceremony and memorial overlays are still never dimmed
The lights-out RULE is untouched: LightsOutRule and US-101's nightly care mistake still turn on LightState, which this story does not change
LightTests still pass; add one asserting the scrim's extent is the sprite slot and not the screen
Tests pass
Verify on the watchOS Simulator: screenshots at on, semi and off on 41mm - in all three the five action buttons and the Dex icon are equally bright, and only the Digimon's area darkens
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: The sleep bar's label is Zz
userStories.description: As a user, I want the sleep bar's label to take no more room than the other three, so the bars get the width instead. EnergyType.shortName returns 'SLEEP' for .spirit - the only 5-character label among STEP / KCAL / EXER. It is not persisted; rawValue is. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-battle-cost-sleep-and-screen-polish.md, US-113.
acceptanceCriteria: EnergyType.spirit.shortName is 'Zz' (two characters, capital Z then lowercase z)
strength / vitality / stamina are UNCHANGED: 'STEP', 'KCAL', 'EXER'
EnergyBarLayout.nameWidth is re-sized for the new longest label (4 characters, not 5) and its comment no longer says 'wide enough for SLEEP'
The width reclaimed goes to the BAR, not to padding - that is the point of the change
No label truncates and all four columns still line up at nameFontSize
VoiceOver is unaffected: the accessibility label is goal.type.displayName, which still says 'Spirit'. A screen reader must NOT be handed 'Zz'
EnergyBarLayout's 'two of these fit the narrowest screen' arithmetic still holds and its test still passes
Check whether any complication view draws shortName; if so, screenshot it too
Test: shortName for all four cases, asserting the new value AND that the other three did not drift
Test: the longest shortName fits nameWidth at nameFontSize
EnergyBarsTests and DominantEnergyTests updated where they assert 'SLEEP'
Tests pass
Verify on the watchOS Simulator: screenshot the energy bars on 41mm (the narrow case the width exists for) and on 46mm
Typecheck passes
